D4 Colorimetry L3 Apply Reference

The Ostwald Solid

slide the hue and read the double-cone cross-section: full colour at the rim, white above, black below

Ostwald built colour from full colour, white, and black content that sum to a whole. Stacking every hue's triangle around a shared white-black axis makes a double cone.

Slide the hue. The diamond is the axial slice through the double cone — this hue on the right, its complement on the left, white at the top apex, black at the bottom. The hue ring marks the pair.
